Committee.CommitteeName: Subcommittee on Appropriations - Health, Mental Health, and Social Services

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 109-I, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Aaron Crossley, a Democrat, represents part of Jackson County (District 29) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first two-year term in November 2022.
In addition to his legislative service, Crossley works as a behavioral health business strategist and project manager at a local safety-net hospital. A Licensed Master Social Worker (LMSW), Crossley has spent two decades in roles focused on social and community service.
Crossley is deeply engaged in his community, having served on several boards and commissions, including the Independence Chamber of Commerce Foundation and others that address health, housing, and the arts. He is also a member of the National Association of Social Workers, the Missouri Cattlemen's Association, and Communications Workers of America (CWA) Local 6400.
He holds degrees from Appalachian Bible College and the University of Missouri-Kansas City.
Born in Independence, Crossley resides there with his wife, Jessica, and their two children. He is an active member of St. Michael’s Episcopal Church.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Firefighters' Procedural Bill of Rights Act"
Modifies provisions relating to catalytic converters

Authorizes the "Child Care Contribution Tax Credit Act", the "Employer-Provided Child Care Assistance Tax Credit Act", and the "Child Care Providers Tax Credit", relating to tax credits for child care
Modifies provisions relating to master's social work degree programs
Modifies standards regarding consumer installment lenders and loans
Specifies that license plate covers must be transparent and non-tinted
Modifies provisions relating to school employee retirement systems
Modifies provisions relating to inspections of facilities licensed by the department of health and senior services
Allows an additional thirty days for individuals sixty-two years of age and older to file property tax assessment appeals
Changes the age when a person may be excused from serving on a petit and grand jury
Modifies provisions relating to the disposition of human remains
Specifies that the seller of ammunition must verify the age of the purchaser
Modifies a provision authorizing a transient guest tax in certain cities
Authorizes the "Missouri Disabled Veterans Homestead Exemption"
Prohibits the state from participating in Daylight Saving Time unless federal law is modified
Designates the "WWII Robert Earl Sauls Memorial Highway" in Jackson County
Designates the "Officer Cody Allen Memorial Highway" in Lafayette County
Establishes provisions relating to the reporting of positive rental payment information to a consumer reporting agency
Creates provisions relating to court-ordered involuntary outpatient treatment for persons with mental disorders
Extends the sunset of the line of duty compensation act
Proposes a constitutional amendment to require Jackson County to have an elected county assessor instead of an unelected assessment department

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Authorizes the "Child Care Contribution Tax Credit Act", the "Employer-Provided Child Care Assistance Tax Credit Act", and the "Child Care Providers Tax Credit", relating to tax credits for child care
Modifies provisions relating to telehealth services
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes an amendment to the Constitution of Missouri to require Jackson County to have an elected assessor
